accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context (e.g., 287(g) as INA provision for state/local immigration enforcement cooperation).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; added context like CBP/ICE/DHS roles and 287(g) description is accurate.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill provides FY2026 supplemental appropriations for border security and immigration enforcement.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly references the statutes invoked (INA section 287(g), the biometric entry-exit system, information-sharing laws).

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the surveillance tower restriction, the qualified cooperating jurisdiction definition, the release prohibition, and the tribal government carve-out.

effective_dates.why: States funds remain available through September 30, 2029, the October 31, 2028 processing coordinator restriction, and the June 10, 2026 approval date.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all major appropriations across both titles with their amounts and purposes.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive tone with no editorializing or advocacy.

coherent.why: Well-organized by title and internally consistent.

concise.why: Efficient with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names CBP, ICE, HSI, DHS Secretary, Office of the Principal Legal Advisor, and INA 287(g).

correct_figures.why: All figures ($9.55B, $7.45B, $108.5M, $3.45B, $2.5B, $13.02B, $31.075B, $350M) and dates match the bill.
